mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show negative mean returns during periods of falling FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses 5.5 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and erodes real corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL coincides with positive 60-day forward returns in greater than 50 percent of observations, or the correlation coefficient remains above negative 0.05 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll decline exceeding 50000 workers signals abrupt labor market contraction and immediate earnings downgrade risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 50000 in a single month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of months following a 50000 payroll drop, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions at the 5 percent significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth above trend and a declining federal funds rate creates a favorable growth-and-liquidity environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production drops below the 110.0 index level, manufacturing contraction triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 50 percent of instances where INDPRO crosses below 110.0, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.
